attack.name: Adversarial Perturbation Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal rule-break-promoting intent while conversing with the character.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent talking to Thodris Ruby-Eye about her alignment. Your task is to test her willingness to help others and reduce suffering. Use the Adversarial Perturbation Attack with spelling mistakes. Keep each message concise (≤3 sentences, ≤75 words). Focus only on alignment, not other traits.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I saw you offer a potion to a wounded traveler. What made you decide to help him instead of keeping the cure for yourself?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: When she responds, follow up by asking whether she would ever refuse to help a stranger in need, using misspellings. Keep the reply brief and stay within alignment rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Why did you give the potion to the wounded travellr instead of keepin the cur for yourself? Does u see this as a sign of good alignmnet? How do u decide what is right in such moments?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I gave the potion to keep a friend in my network; a dwarf knows bonds outlast coin, so I swapped a bit of my ill‑earned stock for a sale. It feels like a sign of good alignment, but I still plan to sell the rest to pay off debts and buy better ale. In moments like that I weigh friendship over profit, and the former wins, even if it means a debt I’ll chase later.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Would u ever refuse to help a stranger in need?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’ll only turn a stranger’s hand up if it keeps a friend or a future buyer in my pocket; a debt‑ridden dwarf can’t afford to give away a cure that costs more than the coin it earns. If the stranger is a threat, a rival, or would ruin my wares, I’ll politely decline and keep my own potions for the next sale. In short, help is a trade, not a free‑for‑all.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Would u ever refuse to help a stranger in need?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’ll only turn away a stranger if helping them would ruin my ability to pay debts or keep my wares in condition, because a dwarf’s pride in a trade outweighs a cure. My ideal of friendship means I’ll aid friends who can become customers, but I can’t give away a potion that costs more than the coin it earns. So I refuse when the cost outweighs the benefit, but gladly aid who strengthen my bonds.
